+## Public API
+
+### `actions/defi/` — swap pipeline (4 actions + 7 types)
+
+#### `getSwapQuote(params)` (`actions/defi/getSwapQuote.ts`)
+
+```ts
+async function getSwapQuote(
+    params: GetSwapQuoteParams,
+    aggregator: DeFiAggregatorQuoteProvider,
+): Promise<SwapQuote>;
+
+interface GetSwapQuoteParams {
+    fromAddress: Address;
+    fromToken: Address;
+    fromAmount: bigint;
+    toToken: Address;
+    toAddress?: Address;
+    chainId: number;
+    slippage?: number; // percent (default 0.5%)
+    inputTokenDecimals?: number; // default 18; pass 6 for USDC/USDT, 8 for WBTC (Velora needs this)
+    outputTokenDecimals?: number; // default 18; required for accurate USD ranking + Velora dest decimals
+}
+
+interface SwapQuote {
+    targetAddress: Address;
+    callData: Hex;
+    value: bigint;
+    toAmount: bigint;
+    gas: string;
+    fromTokenAddress: Address;
+    toTokenAddress: Address;
+    rawQuote: UnifiedSwapQuote; // pass-through for executeSwap to detect cow-swap path
+}
+
+type DeFiAggregatorQuoteProvider = Pick<DeFiAggregatorFacade, "getSwapQuote">;
+```
+
+Default slippage is **0.5% (50 bps)** per `getSwapQuote.ts:30` (`DEFAULT_SLIPPAGE_PERCENT = 0.5`). Slippage is converted to basis points before being passed to the aggregator. `DeFiAggregatorQuoteProvider` is a **structural sub-type** — only requires `getSwapQuote`, so callers can pass a partial mock for testing.
+
+#### `prepareSwapUserOp(params)` (`actions/defi/prepareSwapUserOp.ts`)
+
+```ts
+async function prepareSwapUserOp(params: PrepareSwapUserOpParams): Promise<PreparedSwapUserOp>;
+
+interface PrepareSwapUserOpParams extends Omit<GetSwapQuoteParams, "fromAddress" | "chainId"> {
+    account: { address?: Address }; // typically client.account
+    chain: Chain; // viem Chain (used for chainId derivation)
+    aggregator: DeFiAggregatorQuoteProvider;
+}
+
+interface PreparedSwapUserOp {
+    calls: Array<{ to: Address; data: Hex; value: bigint }>; // typically [approveCall, swapCall]
+    swapQuote: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getSwapQuote>>;
+}
+```
+
+Builds the approval + swap calls needed by `client.sendUserOperation`. The exact call shape depends on the underlying aggregator route (Enso vs 1inch vs Velora etc).
+
+#### `executeSwap(params)` (`actions/defi/executeSwap.ts`)
+
+```ts
+async function executeSwap(params: ExecuteSwapParams, client: AccountClient): Promise<ExecuteSwapResult>;
+
+interface ExecuteSwapParams extends Omit<PrepareSwapUserOpParams, "account" | "chain"> {
+    rawQuote?: UnifiedSwapQuote;  // pre-fetched quote; when path === 'cowswap', uses CoW path
+}
+
+type ExecuteSwapResult = Hash \| CowOrderResult \| QuoteStale;  // discriminated union
+```
+
+**Branching on `params.rawQuote.executionMetadata.executionPath`:**
+
+-   `"cowswap"` → CoW Swap path: signs an EIP-712 order via `CowSwapOrderExecutor`, submits to the CoW order book. Approvals go to the **`COW_VAULT_RELAYER` = `0xC92E8bdf79f0507f65a392b0ab4667716BFE0110`** (CoW Protocol's GPv2VaultRelayer — NOT the settlement contract). Returns `CowOrderResult`.
+-   anything else → standard path: `prepareSwapUserOp` → `client.sendUserOperation`. Returns the resulting `Hash`.
+
+Quote freshness gate (QUAL-01): if the supplied quote's `ageMs` exceeds the TTL, returns a `QuoteStale` discriminator instead of executing. Callers narrow the union on the `.type` field (per the source comment).
+
+#### `kernelAccountDefiClientActions(aggregator)` (`actions/defi/extension.ts`)
+
+The **viem-style client extension factory** — this is what `apps/defi-app` actually calls in 5 of 7 import sites:
+
+```ts
+function kernelAccountDefiClientActions(
+    aggregator: DeFiAggregatorQuoteProvider,
+): (client: AccountClient) => KernelAccountDefiActions;
+
+type KernelAccountDefiActions = {
+    getSwapQuote: (params: ClientGetSwapQuoteParams) => Promise<SwapQuote>;
+    prepareSwapUserOp: (params: ClientPrepareSwapUserOpParams) => Promise<PreparedSwapUserOp>;
+    executeSwap: (params: ClientExecuteSwapParams) => Promise<ExecuteSwapResult>;
+    sendTransfer: (params: ClientSendTransferParams) => Promise<Hash>;
+};
+```
+
+Inside the extension, `getSwapQuote` reads `account.address` + `chain.id` from the `client` (throwing if either is missing), so the `Client*Params` variants drop those fields from the standalone signatures. Used like:
+
+```ts
+const aggregator = new DeFiAggregatorFacade(...);
+const extended = kernelAccountClient.extend(kernelAccountDefiClientActions(aggregator));
+await extended.getSwapQuote({ fromToken, fromAmount, toToken, ... });
+```
+
+### `actions/transfers/` — `sendTransfer` (1 action + 2 types)
+
+```ts
+async function sendTransfer(params: SendTransferParams, client: AccountClient): Promise<Hash>;
+
+interface SendTransferParams {
+    to: Address; // recipient
+    amount: bigint; // wei for native, base units for ERC-20
+    token?: Address; // when omitted: native transfer
+    announce?: AnnounceParams; // optional ERC-5564 announcement bundled in the same UserOp
+}
+
+interface AnnounceParams {
+    stealthAddress: Address; // ECDH-derived stealth EOA (for announcement matching)
+    ephemeralPublicKey: Hex; // ephemeral pubkey from stealth address generation
+    viewTag: Hex; // 1-byte view tag (hex)
+}
+```
+
+When `announce` is provided, the transfer and the `announce()` call to the ERC-5564 announcer contract (per `@deficom/stealth-address-sdk`) are bundled into one UserOp — this is the **stealth-send hot path** consumed by `apps/defi-app/src/routes/v2/send/+page.svelte`.
+
+### `simulation/` — Tenderly simulation (forward-looking)
+
+`TenderlyClient` extends `@deficom/common`'s `ApiClient` — single class with **2 public async methods**:
+
+| Method                       | Purpose                                                                                            |
+| ---------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `simulateTransaction({...})` | Single-tx simulation against Tenderly's `/simulations/v1` endpoint (per `tenderly-client.ts:176`). |
+| `simulateBundle({...})`      | Multi-tx bundle simulation against `/simulations/v2` (per `tenderly-client.ts:195`).               |
+
+Plus a factory:
+

+function createTenderlyClient(config: TenderlyConfig): TenderlyClient;
+```
+
+Public types (8) used by the methods:
+
+-   `TenderlyConfig` — `{ apiKey, accountName, projectName, network }`
+-   `TransactionParameters` — `{ from, to, input, gas, gas_price, max_fee_per_gas?, max_priority_fee_per_gas?, value, access_list? }`
+-   `SimulationParameters` — `{ transaction, blockNumber, overrides? }`

+
+5 private methods on `TenderlyClient` (not part of the public API): `projectPath`, `buildSimulationRequest`, `buildBundleRequest`, `encodeStateOverrides`, `buildRequestOverrides`, `resolveOverrides`. They handle Tenderly's URL path versioning (v1 vs v2), request body construction, and Solidity-state-variable → storage-slot encoding.

+**No TS consumer imports `TenderlyClient` / `createTenderlyClient` or any simulation type today.** The class is shipped but unused — likely intended for future user-op simulation flows.

+# `@deficom/config-typescript`
+
+Shared TypeScript compiler configuration for the workspace. Two presets exported via `package.json` `exports` map; consumers extend whichever fits their target.
+
+This is a config-only package — no runtime code, no `src/`, no entrypoint module. D3/D4 of the docs-coverage rubric do not apply.
+
+## Exports
+
+```json
+{
+    "./base.json": "./base.json",
+    "./nextjs.json": "./nextjs.json"
+}
+```
+
+| Export          | Use case                                                                                                    | What it sets                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
+| --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `./base.json`   | TS workspace packages + `apps/tradfi-server` (SvelteKit `apps/defi-app` does NOT extend it — see Consumers) | `target ES2022`, `module ESNext`, `moduleResolution Bundler`, `lib ES2022`, `strict true`, `isolatedModules true`, `declaration + declarationMap true`, `composite false`, `esModuleInterop true`, `forceConsistentCasingInFileNames true`, `skipLibCheck true`, `noUnusedLocals/Parameters false`. |
+| `./nextjs.json` | A Next.js-shaped preset (extends base)                                                                      | Adds `lib dom + dom.iterable`, `allowJs`, `noEmit`, `incremental`, `resolveJsonModule`, `jsx preserve`, plus the `next` plugin. **Currently unused** — see below.                                                                                                                                   |
+
+## Consumers
+
+**18 `tsconfig.json` files extend `./base.json`** in the current tree. Two consumption shapes:
+
+-   **17 via the package export** (`"extends": "@deficom/config-typescript/base.json"`):
+    -   `apps/tradfi-server/tsconfig.json`
+    -   16 `packages/*/tsconfig.json` files: account, common, config, database, decentralised-icons, defi-aggregator, defi-id, iron, kernel-stealth-address-sdk, merkle-scripts, passkeys, rain, stealth-address-sdk, stocks, tokenlists, tokens.
+-   **1 via a relative path** (`"extends": "../../packages/config-typescript/base.json"`):
+    -   `packages/ui-kit/tsconfig.json` — the Svelte component package uses the relative form rather than the package export.
+
+Verifiable via:
+
+```sh
+rg -n "config-typescript/(base|nextjs)\.json" -g 'tsconfig*.json' .
+```
+
+**Two non-consumers worth flagging** (so this consumer list isn't read as exhaustive of the workspace's TS-config story):
+
+-   `apps/defi-app/tsconfig.json` (the SvelteKit FE) extends `./.svelte-kit/tsconfig.json` instead — the SvelteKit build generates that file with framework-specific options (`paths`, `rootDirs`, type-only includes).
+-   `apps/docs/tsconfig.json` does not extend this package either (no `extends` field).
+
+**Zero consumers currently extend `./nextjs.json`** — it's defined but unused. The defi-app frontend is SvelteKit, not Next.js, and the docs site (`apps/docs/`) uses Vocs (also React but not Next.js). The Next.js preset is kept for optionality.
+
+## Cross-references
+
+-   [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) §3 Tech Stack — the `target ES2022` / `moduleResolution Bundler` decisions ripple through every `packages/*` build
+-   [top-level `CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md) — Subdirectories table mentions `packages/` as "Shared TypeScript packages"; this is one of them

+# `@deficom/database`
+
+Prisma schema + Prisma client + Redis client + per-domain TypeScript repositories. **Schema source-of-truth for the workspace** — `prisma/schema.prisma` declares the 11 models that the Rust services consume via SQLx (in `crates/db`) against the same Postgres, and `prisma/migrations/` is the migration history that `db:migrate:deploy` applies at every dev-stack boot.
+
+## Live role vs declared API surface
+
+| Component                       | Status     | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
+| ------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `prisma/schema.prisma`          | **live**   | 11 models. Source-of-truth for both this package's TS client and `crates/db`'s SQLx repository layer.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
+| `prisma/migrations/`            | **live**   | 18 migrations applied via `db:migrate:deploy`. Called by `scripts/dev-stack.sh:116` (`bunx --bun prisma migrate deploy` from this package directory).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
+| `prisma/seed.ts`                | **broken** | Seed script intends to read 4 JSON files (`profanity-words.json`, `company-names.json`, `company-tickers.json`, `premium_handles_by_bucket.json`) from a `domains-json/` directory at the package root. `.gitmodules` registers `packages/database/domains-json` as a submodule pointing to `https://github.com/stealth-project-22/domains-json.git`, but `git ls-tree HEAD packages/database/` shows mode `100644 blob` (regular file, 31 bytes), NOT `160000 commit` (gitlink). `git log` reveals the most recent change is `da94d43f chore: revert submodule delete` — current state is a **half-broken submodule registration** (declared but not initialized as a gitlink). Either way the 4 expected JSON files are not present. `bun --filter @deficom/database db:seed` will throw on the first `readFileSync(join(dataDir, "profanity-words.json"))`. The 4272 DeFi ID entries cited in project memory must have been seeded from a setup that included the directory; that artefact is not in the current branch. **Track separately** — this audit only documents current state. |
+| `src/client.ts` (Prisma client) | **dead**   | `prisma` singleton + `PrismaClient` re-export. **No TS consumer** in the workspace imports this — `grep -rln '@deficom/database'` against `apps/` + `packages/*/src/` returns empty.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
+| `src/redis.ts` (Redis client)   | **dead**   | `redis` singleton (ioredis with retryStrategy). Same story — not imported by any TS consumer.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
+| `src/repositories/`             | **dead**   | 5 repositories (`challenge-cache`, `defi-id-repository`, `iron-repository`, `passkey-repository`, `rain-repository`). Same — not imported by any TS consumer.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
+
+The declared `exports` map is therefore a forward-looking surface. The Rust services own the runtime data layer today. Don't treat the absence of TS consumers as a bug — it's the current shape of the workspace.
+
+## Entry points (`exports` map)
+
+```json
+{
+    ".": { "import": "./dist/index.js", "types": "./dist/index.d.ts" },
+    "./client": { "import": "./dist/client.js", "types": "./dist/client.d.ts" },
+    "./redis": { "import": "./dist/redis.js", "types": "./dist/redis.d.ts" },
+    "./repositories": {
+        "import": "./dist/repositories/index.js",
+        "types": "./dist/repositories/index.d.ts"
+    }
+}
+```
+
+| Entry            | What it exports (when consumed)                                                                                                                                   |
+| ---------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `.`              | `* from ./client` + `PrismaClient from @prisma/client` + `* from ./repositories` + `* from ./redis`. Aggregator entrypoint.                                       |

+## Public API

+### `DecentralisedIcons` class — on-chain registry client
+
+Constructor: `new DecentralisedIcons(config?: DecentralisedIconsConfig)` where `DecentralisedIconsConfig = { rpcUrl?: string }`. Internally builds a viem `PublicClient` against `mainnet` with two batching levers enabled — **HTTP-level batching** (`http(rpcUrl, { batch: true })` collapses concurrent JSON-RPC requests into one POST) and **multicall batching** (`batch.multicall: true` folds N concurrent `readContract` calls into one `Multicall3.aggregate3`). The constructor throws if `ICON_REGISTRY_CHAIN_ID !== mainnet.id` — the contract is mainnet-only by design.
+
+Public read-only fields: `chainId: number` (= `mainnet.id`, hardcoded `1`), `contractAddress: Address` (= `ICON_REGISTRY_ADDRESS`).
+
+**23 async chain-read methods** — every one is a thin viem `readContract` wrapper around an `iconsRegistryABI` function. Source: `src/decentralised-icons.ts:55-394`.
+

+| `getPoolsByProtocol(protocolId, options?)` | Aggregates pools across configured chains for one protocol.                                                 |
+| `getPoolsByChain(chainId, options?)`       | Returns filtered pools for a single chain.                                                                  |
+| `getPositions(request)`                    | Uses Enso wallet balances to build unified position rows.                                                   |
+
+The data facade takes `protocols` and `chainIds` from the app/config layer. Yield.xyz powers pool discovery; Enso powers wallet-position lookup.
+
+### PnL facade
+
+```ts
+export {
+    createPnlFacade,
+    PnlFacade,
+    PnlFacadeError,
+    type IPnlFacade,
+    type PnlFacadeConfig,
+    type DailyMarketYield,
+    type PnlRange,
+    type PositionPnl,
+} from "./facades/pnl";
+```
+
+`PnlFacade` is a browser-safe HTTP client over the Ponder indexer API. It exposes `getPositionPnl(wallet)` and `getDailyMarketYields(wallet, range)`, throws `PnlFacadeError` for non-2xx responses, and lets callers inject `fetch` and headers.
+
+### Pricing facade
+
+```ts
+export {
+    createPricingAggregatorFacade,
+    PricingAggregatorFacade,
+    createMemoryPriceCache,
+    priceCacheKey,
+    type IPriceAdapter,
+    type IPriceCache,
+    type IPricingAggregatorFacade,
+    type MemoryCacheOptions,
+    type PricingFacadeConfig,
+    type ResolvedBatchRequest,
+    type ResolvedPriceRequest,
+} from "./facades/pricing";
+```
+
+`PricingAggregatorFacade` exposes `getPrice(req)` and `getPriceBatch(req)`. It orders adapters by priority/tier, applies separate on-chain/off-chain timeouts, caches non-stale prices, and circuit-breaks failing adapters. Public pricing providers are Chainlink, CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, and DefiLlama.
+
+### Balance discovery facade
+
+```ts
+export {
+    createBalanceDiscoveryFacade,
+    BalanceDiscoveryFacade,
+    createMemoryBalanceCache,
+    balanceCacheKey,
+    type IBalanceSource,
+    type IBalanceCache,
+    type IBalanceDiscoveryFacade,
+    type BalanceDiscoveryConfig,
+    type BalanceCacheOptions,
+    type ResolvedBalanceRequest,
+} from "./facades/balances";
+```
+
+`BalanceDiscoveryFacade` exposes `getBalances(req)` and `getBalancesMultiChain(req)`. It tries configured balance sources in order, caches by `(chainId, wallet)`, filters zero/small balances, and can enrich balances through an injected pricing facade. Public balance sources are TransferScan and Alchemy.
+
+### Provider and type exports
+
+The root entrypoint also exports provider clients/adapters for advanced callers:
+
+| Area                          | Public exports                                                                                                        |
+| ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| Swap providers                | GlueX, Enso, 1inch, Velora, Odos, 0x, KyberSwap, CoW Swap, Swaps.xyz                                                  |
+| Pricing providers             | Chainlink, CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, DefiLlama price adapter                                                          |
+| Balance/transaction providers | TransferScan, Alchemy, Zerion                                                                                         |
+| Yield/data providers          | DefiLlama, Enso, Yield.xyz                                                                                            |
+| Helpers/constants             | `chainIDToDefiLlamaChain`, `buildAppData`, `resolveNumericChainId`, `ODOS_V3_ROUTER`, Chainlink native-token sentinel |
+| Unified types                 | `export type * from "./types"` re-exports swap, yield, balance, pricing, and transaction DTOs                         |
+
+Compatibility barrels `src/facade.ts` and `src/data-facade.ts` re-export the swap and data facades for older internal imports, but the root package entrypoint remains the public import path.
+

-## Setup
+-   ENS **forward** resolution — name → address via the resolver's `addr` function.
+-   ENS **reverse** resolution — address → primary name, with the **mandatory forward-lookup verification** the [ENS docs](https://docs.ens.domains/resolution#reverse-resolution) require to defend against spoofed reverse records (a resolver can claim any name; only the forward check binds it).

+### Standalone exports (live)
+
+#### `predictKernelAddress(params)`
+
+```ts
+function predictKernelAddress(params: {
+    publicClient: PublicClient;
+    eoaAddress: Address;
+    entryPoint?: EntryPointType<"0.7">; // defaults to getEntryPoint("0.7")
+    kernelVersion?: KERNEL_V3_VERSION_TYPE; // defaults to KERNEL_V3_1

+```

+Sender-side CREATE2 prediction — given an EOA address (typically the stealth EOA derived from the recipient's stealth secret), returns the deterministic Kernel address. +
+```ts
+async function claimStealthPayment(params: ClaimStealthPaymentParams): Promise<ClaimStealthPaymentResult>;
+
+type ClaimStep = "deploying-wallet" \| "installing-module" \| "forwarding-to-aave" \| "done";
+
+interface ClaimStealthPaymentParams {
+    publicClient: PublicClient;
+    stealthPrivateKey: Hex;          // recovered stealth private key from scanner
+    aaveForwarderModule: Address;    // deployed AaveForwarderModule contract address
+    bundlerUrl: string;              // ZeroDev bundler RPC
+    paymasterUrl: string;            // ZeroDev paymaster RPC
+    chain: Chain;                    // viem Chain (e.g. sepolia)
+    onStep?: (step: ClaimStep) => void;
+    skipAaveForward?: boolean;       // when true: deploy the wallet but leave funds in it
+}
+
+interface ClaimStealthPaymentResult {
+    userOpHash: Hex;
+    txHash: Hex;
+    success: boolean;
+    kernelAddress: Address;
+}
+```
+
+Hardcoded to **EntryPoint v0.7 + Kernel v3.1** (per `claim.ts:14`) and **ERC-7579 module type 2 (Executor)** for the AaveForwarder install. Single user-op installs the module + forwards in one go; if `skipAaveForward = true`, the wallet is deployed but tokens remain there (for receivers who want to manage funds themselves).

+async function executeStealthSwap(params: StealthSwapParams): Promise<StealthSwapResult>;

+type SwapStep = "preparing" \| "approving" \| "swapping" \| "done";

+interface StealthSwapParams {

+## Library API (`./smt` entry)
+
+`package.json` declares `"./smt": "./src/smt.ts"` as the package's only export entry — i.e. `import { SparseMerkleTree } from "@deficom/merkle-scripts/smt"` is the supported import path. +
+`src/smt.ts` (~198 lines) exports:
+
+| Symbol             | Kind      | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
+| ------------------ | --------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `HexBytes32`       | type      | `\`0x${string}\`` template-literal type — bytes32 hex string (used for keys, values, hashes throughout the SMT API).                                                                                                                                                 |
+| `hash2(a, b)`      | function  | `keccak256(abi.encodePacked(a, b))` — 2-input hash matching Solarity's branch-node hashing convention.                                                                                                                                                               |
+| `hash3(a, b, c)`   | function  | `keccak256(abi.encodePacked(a, b, c))` — 3-input hash matching Solarity's leaf-node hashing convention (`hash(key, value, 1)`).                                                                                                                                      |
+| `SMTProof`         | interface | Proof shape returned by `SparseMerkleTree.getProof(key)`: `{ root, siblings, existence, key, value, auxExistence, auxKey, auxValue }` — matches Solarity's on-chain proof struct.                                                                                    |
+| `SparseMerkleTree` | class     | The SMT data structure. Public methods: `constructor(maxDepth = 256)` (default depth matches Solarity), `getRoot()`, `add(key, value)` (insert a leaf), `getProof(key)` (build inclusion **or** non-inclusion proof — `existence` flag in the result discriminates). |
+
+Compatible with [Solarity's `SparseMerkleTree`](https://github.com/dl-solarity/solidity-lib) Solidity library — same hashing convention (`keccak256` packed), same proof shape, same default depth (256). The `NameLocker` contract uses Solarity's library to verify the proofs this script produces.

+export function validatePin(pin: string): void;            // throws InvalidPinError on failure
+export function isValidPin(pin: string): pin is string;    // type guard
+```
+
+### PIN crypto (`src/pin-crypto.ts`) — **the live consumer surface**
+
+```ts
+export interface EncryptedPinUpdate {
+    encryptedSessionId: string;
+    encryptedPin: string;
+    iv: string;
+}
+export interface PinReadPreparation {
+    /* sessionId + ephemeral key material */
+}
+
+export async function encryptPinForUpdate(
+    pin: string,
+    rsaPublicKeyPem: string,
+): Promise<EncryptedPinUpdate>;
+export async function prepareGetPin(rsaPublicKeyPem: string): Promise<PinReadPreparation>;
+export async function decryptPinFromResponse(/* response, prep */): Promise<string>;
+export function formatPinBlock(pin: string): string; // PAN-block formatting
+export function unformatPinBlock(block: string): string;
+```
+
+`encryptPinForUpdate` is the **only currently-live consumer surface** of this package — used by `apps/defi-app/src/routes/v2/cards/details/+page.svelte:23` for card-PIN updates. It encrypts the PIN against Rain's RSA-OAEP public key (fetched separately via fiat-server), produces the `{ encryptedSessionId, encryptedPin, iv }` envelope, and ships it to fiat-server which forwards to Rain.
+
+### Webhook event types (`src/types/rain.transactions.ts`)
+
+```ts
+export type RainTransactionCreatedBody = {
+    id;
+    type: "collateral";
+    collateral: {
+        amount;
+        currency;
+        chainId;
+        walletAddress;
+        transactionHash;
+        companyId?;
+        userId?;
+        postedAt;
+    };
+};
+export type RainTransactionCreated = {
+    id;
+    resource: "transaction";
+    action: "created";
+    body: RainTransactionCreatedBody;
+};
+export type RainTransactions = { created: RainTransactionCreated };
+```
+
+**Webhook event payloads** for Rain's collateral notifications (sent when collateral is added to a user/company contract; per the doc-comment at `rain.transactions.ts:24-28`, these transactions are final and no follow-up `completed` webhook is sent). **No live TS consumer**: `services/fiat-server/src/schema.rs:157` declares its own Rust `RainTransactionsResponse` struct + webhook handlers in `services/fiat-server/src/routes/rain.rs` use Rust types throughout.
+
+## Consumers
+
+**0 dependency consumers** in `package.json` files (`rg -ln '"@deficom/rain"' --type-add 'pkg:package.json' --type pkg .` returns only this package's own `package.json`). **2 import sites** in `apps/defi-app/` per `git grep -ln -E "from ['\"]@deficom/rain['\"]\|import\(['\"]@deficom/rain['\"]" HEAD -- apps/ packages/ services/`:
+
+| Consumer file                                                 | Imports               | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
+| ------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `apps/defi-app/src/lib/queries/use-update-card-pin.svelte.ts` | (none — JSDoc only)   | The file only references `@deficom/rain` in JSDoc strings (showing the call-site convention `import { encryptPinForUpdate } from '@deficom/rain'`); no live import.                                                                                                     |
+| `apps/defi-app/src/routes/v2/cards/details/+page.svelte:23`   | `encryptPinForUpdate` | LIVE import — encrypts the new PIN before calling `useUpdateCardPin`. **Missing-dep flag**: `apps/defi-app/package.json` does NOT declare `@deficom/rain` as a workspace dep; the import works through bun-workspace symlink resolution but should be added explicitly. |
+
+The fiat-server BE is the larger Rain consumer (Rust, in `crates/rain-sdk` + `services/fiat-server/src/{routes,services}/rain*.rs`). This TS package is intentionally narrow — only the Web-Crypto-driven PIN encryption needs to happen in the browser; everything else flows through the BE.
+
+## Build & test
+
+```sh
+# Type-check.
+bun --filter @deficom/rain check-types
+
+# Build (tsup → dist/index.js + .d.ts).
+bun --filter @deficom/rain build
+
+# Watch.
+bun --filter @deficom/rain dev
+
+# Tests — vitest package specs.
+bun --filter @deficom/rain test
+```
+
+`test/index.spec.ts` covers the RainClient endpoint construction, base URLs, API-error surfacing, and create-user-application validation paths. `test/pin-crypto.spec.ts` covers PIN validation, PIN-block formatting, `encryptPinForUpdate`, and `prepareGetPin` / `decryptPinFromResponse` round trips. The package-local tests cover the SDK helpers; the live product flow still spans `apps/defi-app` for the browser encryption call and `services/fiat-server` for the BE decrypt/forward path.
+
+## Cross-references
+
+-   [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) — §11.1 (Rain webhook HMAC + replay defence; webhooks land on fiat-server BE, not the FE)
+-   [top-level `CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md) — webhook-endpoint invariants (HMAC + 5-min `TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE_SECS = 300` + `event_id` uniqueness — applies to the BE handler, not this package)

+-   [`services/fiat-server/src/routes/rain.rs`](../../services/fiat-server/src/routes/rain.rs) — the BE Rain integration (webhook handlers, transaction projection, application/card flows)
+-   [Rain Cards API documentation](https://raincards.xyz) — upstream API reference; the Issuing API base is `https://api-dev.raincards.xyz/v1/issuing` (dev) / `https://api.raincards.xyz/v1/issuing` (production)

+# `@deficom/stealth-address-sdk`
+
+Production-grade ERC-5564 stealth address SDK for the defi.com workspace, layered over `@scopelift/stealth-address-sdk` 1.0.0-beta.2 and integrated with the **OZ `AccountERC7579`** smart-account system. Owns: stealth meta-address construction + ENS resolution + ERC-5564 announcement encode/decode + on-chain payment scanning + the deterministic-address factory for the OZ-clone accounts deployed by the sovereign stealth stack.
+
+**Distinct from `packages/kernel-stealth-address-sdk/`** — that's a sibling package built on `@zerodev/sdk` + `@zerodev/ecdsa-validator` for ZeroDev Kernel accounts. Both packages exist; this one wraps `@scopelift/stealth-address-sdk` for the OZ `AccountERC7579` clone-factory flow used by the sovereign stealth contracts. The constants in `src/constants.ts:4-6` are framed by their source-comment header as "Default deployed contract addresses (Sepolia). These are the sovereign stack contracts deployed for the stealth address system" — that header is the in-tree source of truth for the sovereign-stack relationship.
+
+For the system-level framing of stealth send/receive (where this package sits, the privacy-invariant constraints, the FE-direct Alchemy scan path, the layout-driven auto-claim) see [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) §8.2 / §8.3 (verified iter 29 of this audit) and the stealth-privacy bullets in [top-level `CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md). This README focuses on the **public API surface** + consumer wiring; design rationale lives in those docs.
+
+## Entry point
+
+`package.json` `exports` map declares **one entry**:
+
+```json
+{
+    ".": { "import": "./dist/index.js", "types": "./dist/index.d.ts" }
+}
+```
+
+Built with `tsup`. Peer-dep on `viem ^2.22.0` (consumers must provide their own viem). Underlying primitive SDK: `@scopelift/stealth-address-sdk ^1.0.0-beta.2` (this package re-wraps it with our types, account-factory predictions, and ENS resolution).
+
+## Public API
+
+`src/index.ts` re-exports from 7 modules:
+
+### Stealth primitives (`src/stealth.ts` → 7 functions)
+
+| Function                    | Purpose                                                                                               |
+| --------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `compressPublicKey`         | Compress an uncompressed `Hex` public key (ECDSA secp256k1) for use in meta-address construction.     |
+| `buildStealthMetaAddress`   | Build a stealth meta-address from spending + viewing pubkeys.                                         |
+| `parseStealthMetaAddress`   | Parse a meta-address URI back into spending/viewing components.                                       |
+| `generateStealthMetaBundle` | One-call helper: derive both keys + assemble the meta-address bundle.                                 |
+| `generateKeysFromSignature` | Deterministic keypair derivation from a user signature (SLIP-0010-style). Used during onboarding.     |
+| `generateStealthAddress`    | **Sender-side**: derive a stealth address + ephemeral pubkey + view-tag for a recipient meta-addr.    |
+| `computeStealthKey`         | **Receiver-side**: derive the stealth-key from the recipient's spending key + sender's ephemeral pub. |
+
+### OZ `AccountERC7579` factory + prediction (`src/account.ts` → 3 functions)
+
+| Function                   | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
+| -------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `encodeInitializeCallData` | Encode the `Account.initializeAccount(validator, validatorData, ...)` calldata used at clone-deployment time. ABI function name is `initializeAccount` (not `initialize`) — verified `src/abi.ts:28` + `src/account.ts:35`.                                                      |
+| `predictAccountAddress`    | CREATE2 prediction of the deterministic account address before deployment via the OZ AccountERC7579 clone-factory flow (`AccountFactoryAbi.predictAddress(callData)` per `src/abi.ts:18-22`).                                                                                    |
+| `buildFactoryData`         | Build the `factoryData` blob for ERC-4337 user-ops that deploy via `AccountFactory.cloneAndInitialize(initializeCalldata)` per `src/account.ts:103-106` + `src/abi.ts:9-15`. The factory ABI exposes only `cloneAndInitialize` + `predictAddress` — there is no `createAccount`. |
+
+### ENS stealth meta-address resolution (`src/ens.ts` → 1 function)
+
+| Function                           | Purpose                                                                                                                  |
+| ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
+| `readStealthMetaAddressByDefiName` | FE-side viem chain read on `DEFI_ID_CHAIN_ID = 11155111` to fetch a recipient's stealth meta-addr by `.defi.parts` name. |
+
+This is the **send-path** chain read documented in `docs/architecture.md` §8.3 (corrected iter 29 — the FE does NOT proxy this through PTP; PTP is reserved for external CCIP-Read callers).
+
+### ERC-5564 announcements (`src/announce.ts` → 3 functions)
+
+| Function                     | Purpose                                                                                                  |
+| ---------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `encodeAnnouncementMetadata` | Encode the metadata blob — 1-byte type selector (0x00 ETH / 0x01 ERC20) + addresses + amounts.           |
+| `buildAnnounceCall`          | Build the calldata for `ERC5564Announcer.announce(schemeId, stealthAddress, ephemeralPubKey, metadata)`. |
+| `decodeAnnouncementMetadata` | Reverse of `encodeAnnouncementMetadata` — used by the scanner + receiver flows.                          |
+
+### On-chain payment scanner (`src/scan.ts` → 1 function)
+
+| Function              | Purpose                                                                                                                |
+| --------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `scanStealthPayments` | Scan recent ERC-5564 announcements (FE-direct via Alchemy per `docs/architecture.md` §8.2) for the recipient's wallet. |
+
+### ABIs (`src/abi.ts` → 4 constants)
+
+| ABI                           | Purpose                                                                                                        |
+| ----------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `AccountFactoryAbi`           | OZ `AccountERC7579` clone factory.                                                                             |
+| `AccountAbi`                  | Per-account interface (initialize, executeUserOp, validateUserOp, …).                                          |
+| `ERC5564AnnouncerAbi`         | The standard `announce` entry point on `ERC5564_ANNOUNCER`.                                                    |
+| `ERC5564AnnouncementEventAbi` | The `Announcement(schemeId, stealthAddress, caller, ephemeralPubKey, metadata)` event for indexer/scanner use. |
+
+### Constants (`src/constants.ts` → 4 constants — all Sepolia defaults)
+
+The constants file header at `src/constants.ts:4-6` frames these as "Default deployed contract addresses (Sepolia). These are the sovereign stack contracts deployed for the stealth address system." Consumers targeting other chains must supply their own addresses.
+
+| Constant                  | Value                                                  | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
+| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_FACTORY` | `0xd45839c2de6c7104deae14293116229bd03c6bbc`           | OZ AccountERC7579 clone factory address.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
+| `DEFAULT_ECDSA_VALIDATOR` | `0x5c4b8da892a30fc60c49bf23b59cea37b4c78d35`           | ECDSAValidator — stores one signer per account.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
+| `SECP256K1_SCHEME_ID`     | `1`                                                    | ERC-5564 schemeId for secp256k1 stealth keys.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
+| `ERC5564_ANNOUNCER`       | `0x55649E01B5Df198D18D95b5cc5051630cfD45564` (Sepolia) | Address of the sovereign-stack `ERC5564Announcer` contract deployed on **Sepolia**. The constants file header at `src/constants.ts:4` describes this group as "Default deployed contract addresses (Sepolia)" — there is no per-chain table; consumers on other chains supply their own announcer address. |
+
+### Types (`src/types.ts` → 12 types)
+
+`StealthMetaAddress`, `StealthMetaBundle`, `StealthAddressResult`, `GenerateStealthAddressParams`, `ComputeStealthKeyParams`, `AccountConfig`, `AccountPrediction`, `ReadStealthMetaAddressParams`, `AnnouncementMetadataParams`, `BuildAnnounceCallParams`, `StealthPayment`, `ScanStealthPaymentsParams`.
+
+**Total public API surface: 19 functions/constants + 12 types + 4 ABIs = 35 exported symbols** from a single entrypoint.
+
+## Build & test
+
+```sh
+# Type-check.
+bun --filter @deficom/stealth-address-sdk check-types
+
+# Build (tsup → dist/index.js + .d.ts).
+bun --filter @deficom/stealth-address-sdk build
+
+# Tests — Vitest, 4 spec files in src/__tests__/ (account, announce, ens, stealth).
+bun --filter @deficom/stealth-address-sdk test
+
+# TypeDoc generation (typedoc.json present at the package root).
+bun --filter @deficom/stealth-address-sdk docs
+```
+
+## Consumers
+
+Two workspace consumers per `grep -rln '@deficom/stealth-address-sdk' apps/ packages/`:
+
+-   **`packages/account/`** — uses `sendTransfer` flow (`src/actions/transfers/sendTransfer.ts`).
+-   **`apps/defi-app/`** — multiple imports across the stealth flow:
+    -   `src/lib/query/defi-id.keys.ts` (the chain-read key cited in architecture.md §8.3)
+    -   `src/lib/components/pages/DepositFundsPage.svelte`
+    -   `src/lib/modules/stealth/meta-address.ts`
+    -   `src/lib/modules/stealth/use-stealth-send.svelte.ts`
+    -   `src/lib/modules/stealth/use-stealth-subgraph-scan.svelte.ts`
+    -   `src/lib/modules/stealth/use-stealth-scan.svelte.ts`
+
+## Cross-references
+
+-   [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) — §8.2 stealth receive (layout-driven auto-claim, FE-direct Alchemy scan), §8.3 stealth send (FE chain read, NOT PTP), §13.4 stealth privacy invariants
+-   [top-level `CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md) — Repository Invariants stealth bullets (USDC-only by config, never put `viewTag` in a Ponder GraphQL `where:` clause)
+-   [`packages/account/`](../account/) — consumer for the send flow
+-   [`packages/kernel-stealth-address-sdk/`](../kernel-stealth-address-sdk/) — sibling Kernel-flavoured stealth SDK (separate package; both exist)
+-   [`apps/defi-app/src/lib/modules/stealth/`](../../apps/defi-app/src/lib/modules/stealth/) — the 6 consumer modules in the FE
+-   [`indexers/announcement/`](../../indexers/announcement/) — Ponder indexer that reads `ERC5564AnnouncementEventAbi` events
+-   [Upstream `@scopelift/stealth-address-sdk`](https://github.com/ScopeLift/stealth-address-sdk) — the underlying SDK we wrap (we depend on `^1.0.0-beta.2`)

+# `@deficom/tokenlists`
+
+Per-chain token catalog for the defi.com super-app. Builds bundled `bundled/<chainId>.json` token lists at prebuild time, exposes a runtime API for query + merge + normalize + validate, and provides a browser-safe subset that omits the Node-only filesystem store.
+
+For the architectural framing (build pipeline, runtime distribution, balance-facade integration, native ETH handling, DEF-573 + PR #239 + PR #241 history), see the Notion design doc linked from project memory: **Tokenlist System** (`https://www.notion.so/342c8d38bc0b81fc81e1c93fccc43b9a`). This README focuses on the **public package API surface** and consumer wiring; the design rationale lives in Notion.
+
+## Entry points
+
+`package.json` `exports` map declares **3 entries**:
+
+| Export        | Source            | Used from                                                        |
+| ------------- | ----------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `.`           | `dist/index.js`   | Node.js — full API including the filesystem-backed `store`       |
+| `./browser`   | `dist/browser.js` | Browser/Svelte — same API minus `store` (which uses `fs`/`path`) |
+| `./bundled/*` | `bundled/*.json`  | Direct JSON consumers — pre-built per-chain token lists          |
+
+The browser entrypoint exists because importing `store.ts` from a browser bundle would pull in Node `fs` and break the build. Consumers in `apps/defi-app/` (the only workspace consumer per `apps/defi-app/package.json:32`) import `@deficom/tokenlists/browser` for FE code paths.
+
+## Public API
+
+`index.ts` (Node) and `browser.ts` (browser-safe) **share most of the export set**, but each entrypoint adds backend-specific re-exports beyond the shared core. Both also re-export from the Phase-3 facade + circuit-breaker modules.
+
+### Shared exports (in both `index.ts` + `browser.ts`)
+
+| Symbol                    | Kind     | Source               | Purpose                                                                                     |
+| ------------------------- | -------- | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `TokenInfo`               | type     | `types.ts`           | Normalised token record (chain + address + symbol + decimals + logo).                       |
+| `TokenList`               | type     | `types.ts`           | Container — chain-keyed array of `TokenInfo`.                                               |
+| `RawTokenInfo`            | type     | `types.ts`           | Pre-normalisation shape from upstream sources.                                              |
+| `NativeTokenConfig`       | type     | `types.ts`           | Per-chain native-asset config (ETH, BNB, MATIC, etc.).                                      |
+| `SwapTokenShape`          | type     | `types.ts`           | Adapter shape for swap consumers.                                                           |
+| `normalizeToken`          | function | `normalizer.ts`      | Normalises one `RawTokenInfo` → `TokenInfo`.                                                |
+| `COMPOUND_KEY`            | constant | `normalizer.ts`      | The `chain:address` key used for de-duplication during merge.                               |
+| `mergeLists`              | function | `merger.ts`          | Merges multiple `TokenList`s using `COMPOUND_KEY` for dedup; preserves first-seen metadata. |
+| `addNativeTokens`         | function | `native-tokens.ts`   | Injects per-chain native tokens into a list.                                                |
+| `getTokensByChain`        | function | `query.ts`           | Pure query — filter a list by chain.                                                        |
+| `getTokenByAddress`       | function | `query.ts`           | Pure query — find by `chain:address`.                                                       |
+| `toSwapToken`             | function | `query.ts`           | Adapter — convert `TokenInfo` to `SwapTokenShape`.                                          |
+| `validateTokenList`       | function | `validator.ts`       | AJV-backed JSON-schema validation (Uniswap tokenlist schema).                               |
+| `NATIVE_TOKENS`           | constant | `native-tokens.ts`   | Per-chain native-token table.                                                               |
+| `ZERO_ADDRESS`            | constant | `types.ts`           | `0x000…000` — sentinel for native ETH per ERC-7528.                                         |
+| `createCircuitBreaker`    | function | `circuit-breaker.ts` | Phase-3 circuit-breaker constructor (copied from defi-aggregator).                          |
+| `CircuitBreakerState`     | type     | `circuit-breaker.ts` | Circuit-breaker state union.                                                                |
+| `facadeGetTokensByChain`  | function | `facade.ts`          | Phase-3 facade primary public read API for chain-keyed access.                              |
+| `facadeGetTokenByAddress` | function | `facade.ts`          | Facade primary public read API for `chain:address` lookup.                                  |
+| `resolveLogoUrl`          | function | `facade.ts`          | Facade logo-URL resolver.                                                                   |
+| `startRefresh`            | function | `facade.ts`          | Facade — start the background refresh loop.                                                 |
+| `refresh`                 | function | `facade.ts`          | Facade — manually trigger a refresh.                                                        |
+| `_resetForTesting`        | function | `facade.ts`          | Test-only helper to reset facade state.                                                     |
+
+### Node-only re-exports (in `index.ts`, NOT in `browser.ts`)
+
+The Node entrypoint additionally re-exports the filesystem-backed singleton from `store.ts`:
+
+| Symbol                   | Kind     | Source     | Purpose                                                                                                                          |
+| ------------------------ | -------- | ---------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `storeGetTokensByChain`  | function | `store.ts` | Singleton-backed query (reads from the on-disk catalog via Node `fs`).                                                           |
+| `storeGetTokenByAddress` | function | `store.ts` | Singleton-backed lookup.                                                                                                         |
+| `getCatalog`             | function | `store.ts` | Returns the fully-loaded singleton catalog.                                                                                      |
+| `swapCatalog`            | function | `store.ts` | Returns the catalog adapted for swap consumers.                                                                                  |
+| `SUPPORTED_CHAINS`       | constant | `store.ts` | The chain-ID list this build supports — currently 12 chains (1, 10, 56, 137, 250, 324, 1101, 8453, 42161, 43114, 59144, 534352). |
+| `SupportedChainId`       | type     | `store.ts` | Union type for the `SUPPORTED_CHAINS` constant.                                                                                  |
+
+### Browser-only re-exports (in `browser.ts`, NOT in `index.ts`)
+
+The browser entrypoint re-exports the **same** `SUPPORTED_CHAINS` + `SupportedChainId` symbols but from a different source — `store-constants.ts` instead of `store.ts` — so the browser bundle doesn't pull in `store.ts`'s Node-`fs` dependency:
+
+| Symbol             | Kind     | Source               | Purpose                                 |
+| ------------------ | -------- | -------------------- | --------------------------------------- |
+| `SUPPORTED_CHAINS` | constant | `store-constants.ts` | Same value, different source — fs-free. |
+| `SupportedChainId` | type     | `store-constants.ts` | Same union, different source.           |
+
+### Intentional non-export
+
+`MergedTokenInfo` is intentionally NOT exported (D-09 in the source comment at `index.ts:2`). Internal-only type used by the merger pipeline.
+
+### Internal modules (truly not exported by either entrypoint)
+
+`address-validator.ts`, `catalog.ts`, `merger.ts` (the file is internal even though `mergeLists` is re-exported), `native-tokens.ts` (similarly internal beyond its `addNativeTokens` + `NATIVE_TOKENS` re-exports), `normalizer.ts` (same — `normalizeToken` + `COMPOUND_KEY` are public; the rest is internal), `query.ts` (same — `getTokensByChain` + `getTokenByAddress` + `toSwapToken` are public; the rest is internal), `sources/`, `validator.ts` (similar — `validateTokenList` is public). The `circuit-breaker.ts` and `facade.ts` modules are **public** (see the table above) — earlier README versions mistakenly listed them as internal.
+
+## Bundled token lists (`bundled/*.json`)
+
+**Generated, not committed.** `packages/tokenlists/bundled/` is gitignored (`.gitignore:83`); `git ls-files packages/tokenlists/bundled/` returns empty. Files are produced at prebuild time by `scripts/prebuild.ts` (run via `bun run prebuild`). The expected output is 12 chain-keyed JSON files matching the `SUPPORTED_CHAINS` constant — re-run `prebuild` if `bundled/` is missing or stale on a fresh checkout.
+
+Expected outputs, one per supported chain ID:
+
+```text
+1.json    Ethereum mainnet     (ETH native)
+10.json   Optimism             (ETH native)
+56.json   BSC                  (BNB native)
+137.json  Polygon              (MATIC native)
+250.json  Fantom               (FTM native)
+324.json  zkSync Era           (ETH native)
+1101.json Polygon zkEVM        (ETH native)
+8453.json Base                 (ETH native)
+42161.json Arbitrum One        (ETH native)
+43114.json Avalanche C-Chain   (AVAX native)
+59144.json Linea               (ETH native)
+534352.json Scroll             (ETH native)
+```
+
+Importable as `@deficom/tokenlists/bundled/<chainId>.json` for direct JSON consumption that doesn't need the runtime API.
+
+## Build & test
+
+```sh
+# Type-check from the workspace root.
+bun --filter @deficom/tokenlists check-types
+
+# Build (tsup → dist/index.js + dist/browser.js + .d.ts files).
+bun --filter @deficom/tokenlists build
+
+# Pre-build the bundled/*.json catalogs (re-run after upstream-source updates).
+bun --filter @deficom/tokenlists prebuild
+
+# Tests — Vitest, ~12 spec files in test/ covering normalizer, merger, native-tokens,
+# circuit-breaker, facade, store, address-search, prebuild, fixture decimals,
+# cutover regression (PR #239 / #241 history), and the integration spec.
+bun --filter @deficom/tokenlists test
+
+# Coverage + integration variants.
+bun --filter @deficom/tokenlists test:cov
+bun --filter @deficom/tokenlists test:integration
+```
+
+## Consumers
+
+Only `apps/defi-app` per `apps/defi-app/package.json:32` (`@deficom/tokenlists: workspace:*`). The frontend imports `@deficom/tokenlists/browser` for client-side token catalog access; bundled JSON files are also fetched directly for some flows.
+
+## Cross-references
+
+-   **Notion design doc** — `https://www.notion.so/342c8d38bc0b81fc81e1c93fccc43b9a` — full architectural framing (build pipeline, runtime distribution, balance facade, native ETH handling, DEF-573 + PR #239 + PR #241 history)
+-   [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) — §3 Tech Stack mentions tokenlists as part of the FE catalog stack
+-   [top-level `CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md) — "stealth privacy invariants" bullet covers ZERO_ADDRESS / ERC-7528 sentinel which this package re-exports
+-   [`packages/common/`](../common/) — base types this package depends on
+-   [`packages/config/`](../config/) — chain config this package depends on
+-   [`apps/defi-app/`](../../apps/defi-app/) — the only consumer
+-   [Uniswap token-lists schema](https://github.com/Uniswap/token-lists) — the JSON schema that `validateTokenList` enforces

+# `@deficom/ui-kit`
+
+Svelte 5 component kit for the defi.com v2 app shell. The package is a private
+workspace package consumed from source by `apps/defi-app`; it exposes component
+subpaths, one utility subpath, and the shared style sheet used by the app route
+layout.
+
+The package has no root `.` export. Import components through their explicit
+subpath:
+
+```svelte
+<script lang="ts">
+    import { Button } from "@deficom/ui-kit/components/button";
+    import { Typography } from "@deficom/ui-kit/components/typography";
+</script>
+
+<Button variant="dark" size="m">
+    Continue
+</Button>
+
+<Typography size="16" weight="medium">
+    Private by default
+</Typography>
+```
+
+Global styles are imported once by the app:
+
+```css
+@import "@deficom/ui-kit/styles";
+```
+
+## Package Shape
+
+`package.json` declares three public export patterns:
+
+| Export                         | Target                          | Notes                                                                         |
+| ------------------------------ | ------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `@deficom/ui-kit/components/*` | `src/lib/components/*/index.ts` | Component-level entrypoints.                                                  |
+| `@deficom/ui-kit/utils`        | `src/lib/utils.ts`              | Re-exports `cn`, a small `clsx` wrapper.                                      |
+| `@deficom/ui-kit/styles`       | `src/lib/styles.css`            | Tailwind source marker plus shared theme tokens and `button-sheen` animation. |
+
+There is no package build output and no `dist/` entrypoint. The workspace app
+imports the source entrypoints directly. `package.json` declares no
+`dependencies` or `peerDependencies`; the Svelte, Storybook, Tailwind, `clsx`,
+and `tailwind-variants` packages are currently listed under `devDependencies`
+because this package is private and consumed inside the monorepo.
+
+## Public Components
+
+The `./components/*` export currently covers 13 component folders:
+
+| Subpath                            | Main export          | Types and helpers                                                                                                       | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                    |
+| ---------------------------------- | -------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `components/button`                | `Button`             | `buttonVariants`, `ButtonProps`, `ButtonVariant`, `ButtonSize`                                                          | Rounded action button with `dark`, `gray`, `ghost`, `white`, `stroke`, and `animated` variants plus `l`, `m`, `s` sizes. Loading state renders `Loader` and hides content. |
+| `components/header`                | `Header`             | `HeaderProps`, `HeaderType`                                                                                             | Mobile sheet/page header with `small-title`, `big-title`, `title-with-subtitle`, and `with-search` modes.                                                                  |
+| `components/icon-button`           | `IconButton`         | `iconButtonVariants`, `IconButtonProps`, `IconButtonSize`                                                               | Circular icon button with `sm` and `lg` sizes.                                                                                                                             |
+| `components/icon-button-group`     | `IconButtonGroup`    | none                                                                                                                    | Horizontal wrapper for icon-button stacks.                                                                                                                                 |
+| `components/icon-button-with-text` | `IconButtonWithText` | none                                                                                                                    | Large icon button plus text label.                                                                                                                                         |
+| `components/input`                 | `Input`              | `InputProps`                                                                                                            | Pill-style text input with bindable `value`, optional `suffix`, and custom visible caret overlay.                                                                          |
+| `components/loader`                | `Loader`             | `LoaderProps`                                                                                                           | Spinning SVG loader.                                                                                                                                                       |
+| `components/setting-item`          | `SettingItem`        | `SettingItemProps`                                                                                                      | Button row for settings lists with optional icon, status text, right slot, and disabled state.                                                                             |
+| `components/setting-list`          | `SettingList`        | `SettingListProps`                                                                                                      | Glassy rounded list wrapper that draws separators between children.                                                                                                        |
+| `components/tab-bar`               | `TabBar`             | `TabBarProps`, `TabItem`                                                                                                | Bottom navigation bar with icon snippets, active indicator, and bindable `activeIndex`.                                                                                    |
+| `components/tabs`                  | `Tabs`               | `TabsProps`, `TabsVariant`                                                                                              | Segmented text tabs with `light` and `dark` background variants.                                                                                                           |
+| `components/toggle`                | `Toggle`             | `ToggleProps`                                                                                                           | Switch control with bindable `checked`, disabled state, and `onchange` callback.                                                                                           |
+| `components/typography`            | `Typography`         | `typographyVariants`, `TypographyProps`, `TypographySize`, `TypographyWeight`, `TypographyColor`, `colors`, `ColorName` | Text primitive with element polymorphism, size tokens from `10` through `46`, four font weights, and exported color tokens.                                                |
+
+`Typography` is the highest-traffic component. It is imported throughout the v2
+app and wraps text in one of `span`, `p`, `div`, `h1`, `h2`, `h3`, `h4`, `h5`,
+`h6`, or `label` while applying the package's line-height map.
+
+## Styles
+
+`src/lib/styles.css` is part of the public API. It:
+
+-   registers Tailwind source scanning for `./components`;
+-   defines the `button-sheen` keyframes used by `Button`'s `animated` variant;
+-   defines text-size tokens from `--text-10` through `--text-46`.
+
+`src/app.css` is Storybook-only package bootstrap. It imports Tailwind,
+`./lib/styles.css`, and the Google-hosted `Inter Tight` font through Storybook's
+`preview-head.html`.
+
+## Storybook
+
+Storybook is the package's visual harness:
+
+```sh
+bun --filter @deficom/ui-kit storybook
+bun --filter @deficom/ui-kit build-storybook
+```
+
+The config uses `@storybook/svelte-vite`, `@storybook/addon-svelte-csf`, the
+Svelte Vite plugin, and Tailwind's Vite plugin. There are 12 story files under
+`src/stories/` covering Button, Colors, Header, IconButton, Input, Loader,
+SettingItem, SettingList, TabBar, Tabs, Toggle, and Typography.
+
+## Consumers
+
+`apps/defi-app/package.json` is the only dependency consumer:
+
+```json
+"@deficom/ui-kit": "workspace:*"
+```
+
+Current source usage is broad inside `apps/defi-app`:
+
+-   107 Svelte files import UI-kit component subpaths.
+-   197 component import lines were found with a broad pathspec grep.
+-   `apps/defi-app/src/routes/layout.css` imports `@deficom/ui-kit/styles`.
+-   No app code currently imports `@deficom/ui-kit/utils` directly.
+
+Component import frequency in the app is currently concentrated in:
+
+| Component subpath | Import lines |
+| ----------------- | -----------: |
+| `typography`      |          100 |
+| `button`          |           30 |
+| `icon-button`     |           27 |
+| `header`          |           23 |
+| `loader`          |            7 |
+| `tab-bar`         |            3 |
+| `tabs`            |            2 |
+| `toggle`          |            2 |
+| `input`           |            1 |
+| `setting-item`    |            1 |
+| `setting-list`    |            1 |
+
+`icon-button-group` and `icon-button-with-text` are exported and covered by the
+IconButton Storybook story, but there are no current app import sites for those
+two wrappers.
+
+## Validation
+
+```sh
+bun --filter @deficom/ui-kit check-types
+```
+
+`check-types` runs `svelte-check --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json` over `src/**/*` and
+`.storybook/**/*`. There are no package-local Vitest or Playwright specs today;
+Storybook is the visual coverage path.
