+# `services/fiat-server`
+
+Axum + SQLx Rust service that proxies the **Iron** and **Rain** APIs and integrates **Sumsub** KYC. Originally a Bun + Hono service under `apps/fiat-server/`; rewritten in Rust for compile-time DB query verification, MQ-backed webhook fan-out, and shared-pool typed clients. The legacy `apps/fiat-server/` directory is empty post-`a54c3905` (2026-03-23) — this is the canonical implementation.
+
+Default HTTP port: `3001` (override via `PORT`). Process role is selected at startup via `FIAT_ROLE` (`http` / `worker` / `all`, default `all`); the same binary is deployed in either role, and infra runs a separate `fiat-server-worker` Container App with `FIAT_ROLE=worker` (always-on min 1 / max 3 replicas — see `docs/architecture.md` §10 for the full topology).
+
+For the full system context (where this service sits, who calls it, security boundaries) see [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) — specifically §1.1 (system diagram), §5.2 (HTTP endpoint inventory), §10.1 (env vars + Container Apps wiring), §11.1 (trust boundaries + webhook signature shapes), and §13 (the surprising-things bullets that touch this service).
+
+## Binaries
+
+`Cargo.toml` declares three binaries — a main server plus two webhook-replay helpers. The Docker image additionally bundles a fourth binary (`dlq-drain`) built from the sibling `mq` crate; see "Container build" below.
+
+| Binary           | Path                        | Purpose                                                                                            |
+| ---------------- | --------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `fiat-server`    | `src/main.rs`               | Main HTTP server + MQ consumers (Iron, Rain, Sumsub webhook queues). Selects role via `FIAT_ROLE`. |
+| `sumsub-webhook` | `src/bin/sumsub_webhook.rs` | Replay/inspect a single Sumsub webhook payload via the same processor the live server uses.        |
+| `rain-webhook`   | `src/bin/rain_webhook.rs`   | Same as above for Rain webhook payloads.                                                           |
+
+Both helper binaries import from `lib.rs` so the processor logic compiles once across all three.
+
+## Module layout (`src/`)
+
+```text
+auth.rs       Frontend → fiat-server credentialId lookup (see DEF-652 / architecture.md §11.1).
+cache.rs      Redis (deadpool-redis); uses logical DB 1 to isolate from passkey-server.
+config.rs     `Config::from_env()` — single load point for every env var; FiatRole enum lives here.
+crypto.rs     HMAC + signature helpers shared across Iron, Rain, Sumsub.
+error.rs      `AppError` (axum IntoResponse).
+openapi.rs    utoipa `FiatServerApi` aggregator. Mounted twice in `src/main.rs:255-260`: full spec at `/api-docs` and an Iron-only sub-spec at `/api/iron/swagger-ui`. See `--print-spec` flag.
+routes/       Axum route modules — see "Routes" below.
+schema.rs     Per-module `From`/derive helpers used by openapi.rs.
+services/     Long-running domain logic (webhook processors, Sumsub client, scheduler).
+state.rs      `AppState` shared across handlers (DB pool, Redis pool, Iron/Rain/Sumsub clients).
+```
+
+### Routes (`src/routes/`)
+
+| Module               | Mount                 | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
+| -------------------- | --------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `defi_id.rs`         | `/api/defi-id/*`      | DeFi ID claim/status proxy.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
+| `iron.rs`            | `/api/iron/*`         | Iron client + customer/account/autoramp endpoints. See architecture.md §5.2 for the per-handler 503 conditions.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
+| `iron_sandbox.rs`    | `/api/iron/sandbox/*` | Sandbox-only flow helpers. Gated by `ENABLE_SANDBOX` env var (`cfg.enable_sandbox` in `src/config.rs:137-139`; nested via `app.nest("/api/iron/sandbox", ...)` in `src/main.rs:245-247`). Note open security item — see Repository Invariants in [`CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md) and DEF-577 history.                                                                  |
+| `iron_webhooks.rs`   | `/webhooks/iron`      | HMAC-SHA256 (standardwebhooks crate) + `TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE_SECS = 300` + `event_id` uniqueness — the canonical webhook shape from CLAUDE.md Repository Invariants.                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
+| `rain.rs`            | `/api/rain/*`         | Rain SDK proxy.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
+| `rain_webhooks.rs`   | `/webhooks/rain`      | Raw-body HMAC-SHA256 (body-signed) + signed-timestamp freshness — `eventReceivedAt` or fallback `timestamp` parsed from the signed JSON, then 5-min `TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE_SECS` check (`src/routes/rain_webhooks.rs:110-137`) + `event_id` ON CONFLICT idempotency on `rain_webhook_events`. Distinct from Iron's standardwebhooks-header timestamp + signature shape. |
+| `sumsub.rs`          | `/api/sumsub/*`       | WebSDK access token, applicant status, identity reads, Rain-application bridge.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
+| `sumsub_webhooks.rs` | `/webhooks/sumsub`    | `X-Payload-Digest` HMAC only — **no timestamp window**; replay defence is the synthesised `dedup_key` (`applicantId+type+createdAtMs`). See architecture.md §5.2 / §7.1.                                                                                                                                                                                              |
+
+### Services (`src/services/`)
+
+| Module                        | Purpose                                                                                                              |
+| ----------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `iron_webhook_processor.rs`   | Consumes the Iron webhook queue (azqueue); idempotent state updates against `iron_webhook_events`.                   |
+| `rain_webhook_processor.rs`   | Consumes the Rain webhook queue; populates `rain_transactions` (raw-SQL table per migration `20260415120000`).       |
+| `sumsub_webhook_processor.rs` | Consumes the Sumsub webhook queue; idempotent KYC state writes via the synthesised `dedup_key`.                      |
+| `sumsub_client.rs`            | Outbound Sumsub HTTP client — HMAC-signed via `X-App-Token` + `X-App-Access-Ts` + `X-App-Access-Sig` per provider.   |
+| `rain_application.rs`         | Sumsub → Rain share-token bridging (only step that needs both clients live).                                         |
+| `scheduler.rs`                | 180-second stale-claim scheduler for the three webhook-event tables (see migration `20260323000000_add_claimed_at`). |
+
+## Build & run
+
+The Rust workspace requires either an online `DATABASE_URL` or the cached `.sqlx/` metadata for SQLx macros — see the SQLx invariant in [`CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md). CI runs `SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo build` against the committed cache.
+
+```bash
+# Type-check from the workspace root (uses .sqlx/ offline cache).
+SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo check -p fiat-server
+
+# Run the main server (needs all required env vars set — see architecture.md §10.1).
+cargo run --bin fiat-server
+
+# Print the OpenAPI spec to stdout without binding the port.
+cargo run --bin fiat-server -- --print-spec
+
+# Run the full stack locally (Postgres + Redis + Azurite + Rust services + indexer + FE).
+bun run dev:stack
+```
+
+The full local stack is the recommended dev path — `dev:stack` (= `./scripts/dev-stack.sh`) brings up the infra dependencies via Docker, runs Prisma migrations against the local Postgres, then starts this service alongside passkey-server, the Ponder indexer, and the SvelteKit FE.
+
+## Tests (`tests/`)
+
+| File                          | Coverage                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
+| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
+| `smoke.rs`                    | End-to-end happy-path probe of the Axum router.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
+| `cache_integration.rs`        | Redis round-trips against the FiatCache wrapper.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
+| `iron_webhook_integration.rs` | HMAC verification + timestamp tolerance + replay defence for the Iron webhook handler.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
+| `rain_webhook_integration.rs` | Raw-body HMAC verification + signed-timestamp freshness window (`TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE_SECS = 300` against `eventReceivedAt` / fallback `timestamp` fields parsed from the signed JSON) + `event_id` ON CONFLICT idempotency on `rain_webhook_events`. Distinct from Iron's standardwebhooks-header timestamp + signature shape. |
+| `security.rs`                 | Defensive checks (CORS, header sanitisation, …).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
+| `shared_lock.rs`              | Scheduler / `claimed_at` semantics for the 180-second stale-claim window.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
+
+```bash
+# Single test file
+cargo test -p fiat-server --test smoke
+
+# Full suite
+cargo test -p fiat-server
+```
+
+Integration tests that hit Postgres assume a live `DATABASE_URL` and the dev-stack schema (per `bun run dev:stack`).
+
+## Container build
+
+`Dockerfile` is a 4-stage cargo-chef build (chef → planner → builder → runtime); `RUST_VERSION=1.88` (matches the CI toolchain pin in `.github/workflows/rust.yml`); `CARGO_CHEF_VERSION=0.1.77`; final image is `debian:bookworm-slim` carrying **four binaries** copied from the builder stage: `fiat-server` (default `CMD`), `rain-webhook` and `sumsub-webhook` (helper replay binaries declared in this crate's `Cargo.toml`), and `dlq-drain` (operator CLI from the `mq` crate at `crates/mq/src/bin/dlq_drain.rs` — built via `cargo build --release --package mq --bin dlq-drain --features azqueue` in the builder stage so operators can drain `*-webhooks-dlq` against a running fiat-server deployment). The build does **not** require a live `DATABASE_URL` because the committed `.sqlx/` cache satisfies the SQLx macros at compile time. The MQ backend feature (`azqueue`) is selected at compile time from `Cargo.toml` — no build arg needed.
+
+The image is pushed to `<env>acr.azurecr.io/fiat-server` by `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` and rolled out to two Container Apps (`fiat-server` HTTP + `fiat-server-worker`) plus the optional `fiat-webhook-sync` Job. See [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) §9.2 (CI) and §10 (deployment).
+
+## Cross-references
+
+-   [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) — full system reference (anchor sections noted above)
+-   [`CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md) — Repository Invariants (webhook signature shape, SQLx offline mode, Iron host)
+-   [`crates/iron-client/`](../../crates/iron-client/) — typed Iron HTTP client used here
+-   [`crates/rain-sdk/`](../../crates/rain-sdk/) — typed Rain SDK (workspace member; replaced the legacy `crates/rain-client`)
+-   [`crates/db/`](../../crates/db/) — SQLx repository layer + the `iron`/`rain`/`sumsub` migration helpers called from `main.rs`
+-   [`crates/mq/`](../../crates/mq/) — message-queue abstraction; this service pins `default-features = false, features = ["azqueue"]`
+-   [`packages/database/prisma/schema.prisma`](../../packages/database/prisma/schema.prisma) — model definitions (Prisma owns the schema; SQLx reads it)

+# `services/passkey-server`
+
+Axum + SQLx Rust WebAuthn relying-party (RP) implementation. Originally a Bun + Hono service under `apps/passkey-server/`; rewritten in Rust for compile-time DB query verification and shared-pool challenge management. The legacy `apps/passkey-server/` directory is empty post-`a54c3905` (2026-03-23) — this is the canonical implementation.
+
+Default HTTP port: `8080` (override via `PORT`). Single binary, single process — no role split (unlike fiat-server's `FIAT_ROLE`).
+
+For the full system context (where this service sits, who calls it, the WebAuthn → ZeroDev Kernel chain) see [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) — specifically §1.1 (system diagram), §5 (endpoint inventory), §10.1 (env vars + Container Apps wiring), §11.1 (trust boundaries), and §13 (the surprising-things bullets — including the Redis DB-0 / DB-1 split with fiat-server).
+
+## Binary
+
+`Cargo.toml` declares one binary:
+
+| Binary           | Path          | Purpose                                                                                   |
+| ---------------- | ------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `passkey-server` | `src/main.rs` | HTTP server. Mounts WebAuthn registration + login + signing endpoints + `/health` probes. |
+
+There is **no `src/lib.rs`** — this is a binary-only crate, so D3 (pub-item docs) and D4 (lib-overview + worked example) of the docs-coverage rubric do not apply. Helper binaries can be split out later if needed (the fiat-server pattern), but the current shape is single-binary by design.
+
+## Module layout (`src/`)
+
+```text
+challenge.rs   Redis-backed WebAuthn challenge cache (TTL configured via WEBAUTHN_CHALLENGE_TTL).
+config.rs      `Config::from_env()` — port, DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, CORS_ORIGIN, challenge TTL.
+error.rs       `AppError` (axum IntoResponse).
+main.rs        Bootstrap — DB pool, Redis pool, axum router, CORS, tracing, OpenAPI mount.
+openapi.rs     utoipa `PasskeyServerApi` aggregator; mounted at `/swagger-ui` (see `--print-spec` flag).
+routes.rs      Axum router builder + handler functions (8 endpoints — see "Routes" below).
+schema.rs      `From`/derive helpers used by openapi.rs.
+util.rs        Shared helpers (base64url, RP-ID derivation, …).
+webauthn.rs    `webauthn-rs` integration — registration + authentication options + verifications.
+```
+
+## Routes
+
+```text
+POST /api/v3/register/options    — WebAuthn registration: server-side challenge + RP options.
+POST /api/v3/register/verify     — WebAuthn registration: verify attestation + persist credential.
+POST /api/v3/login/options       — WebAuthn authentication: server-side challenge + allowCredentials.
+POST /api/v3/login/verify        — WebAuthn authentication: verify assertion + return user/session.
+POST /api/v3/sign-initiate       — Initiate a transaction signing flow (WebAuthn-backed).
+POST /api/v3/sign-verify         — Verify the resulting assertion against a pending sign request.
+GET  /api/v3/health              — Versioned health probe.
+GET  /health                     — Generic health probe (Container App liveness/readiness).
+```
+
+The `register` + `login` flows follow the standard WebAuthn options/verify split. `sign-initiate` + `sign-verify` extend the same pattern to per-transaction signing — the FE calls `sign-initiate` with the message hash, gets back a challenge, prompts the user with WebAuthn, then calls `sign-verify` with the assertion.
+
+## Cryptographic surface
+
+-   **WebAuthn RP** via `webauthn-rs` 0.5 (and `webauthn-rs-proto`). The Solady P256 verifier bytecode invariant is a contract-side concern (see `packages/contracts/test/` for the foundry-test invariant); on the server side, `p256 = { features = ["ecdsa"] }` is used directly for assertion checks where `webauthn-rs` doesn't cover them.
+-   **Challenge storage**: Redis. Each registration / login / sign request gets a fresh challenge written to Redis with a TTL of `WEBAUTHN_CHALLENGE_TTL` (milliseconds; the field type is `u64` so the env var is parsed as ms). Per the surprising-things bullet in `docs/architecture.md` §13 / `CLAUDE.md` Repository Invariants: passkey-server uses **Redis DB 0**; fiat-server uses **Redis DB 1** to isolate caches.
+-   **CBOR**: `serde_cbor_2` for attestation parsing.
+
+## Env vars
+
+`Config::from_env()` reads exactly five vars; secrets-derivation (KV pulls in production) is the deploy concern, not a service concern.
+
+| Var                      | Required | Default        | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
+| ------------------------ | -------- | -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `PORT`                   | no       | `8080`         | HTTP listen port.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
+| `DATABASE_URL`           | yes      | —              | Postgres URL. SQLx macros verify queries against `.sqlx/` cache offline.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
+| `REDIS_URL`              | no       | (loopback)     | Redis URL. Per system invariant the URL must point at logical DB 0.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
+| `CORS_ORIGIN`            | yes      | `*` (rejected) | Specific origin (single value parsed via `HeaderValue`). The wildcard default is set by `Config::from_env` but `main.rs:74-76` panics on startup if `CORS_ORIGIN.trim()` equals `*`, so any successful run must override it. The README's earlier "no/dev-only" framing was wrong — operationally this var is required. |
+| `WEBAUTHN_CHALLENGE_TTL` | no       | (lib-default)  | Challenge TTL in ms. Higher means longer prompt-to-verify windows; lower is safer.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
+
+For the full deployed env set (Key Vault references, Container Apps wiring, secret refresh quirks) see `docs/architecture.md` §10.1.
+
+## Build & run
+
+The Rust workspace requires either an online `DATABASE_URL` or the cached `.sqlx/` metadata for SQLx macros — see the SQLx invariant in [`CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md). CI runs `SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo build` against the committed cache.
+
+```bash
+# Type-check from the workspace root (uses .sqlx/ offline cache).
+SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo check -p passkey-server
+
+# Run the server (needs DATABASE_URL + REDIS_URL + a non-wildcard CORS_ORIGIN — see env table; main.rs:74-76 panics on `*`).
+cargo run --bin passkey-server
+
+# Print the OpenAPI spec to stdout without binding the port.
+cargo run --bin passkey-server -- --print-spec
+
+# Run the full stack locally (Postgres + Redis + Azurite + Rust services + indexer + FE).
+bun run dev:stack
+```
+
+## Tests (`tests/`)
+
+| File               | Coverage                                                                                                                   |
+| ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `smoke.rs`         | End-to-end happy-path probe of the Axum router (health + register-options + login-options round-trips).                    |
+| `authenticator.rs` | WebAuthn assertion construction + verification fixtures using a software authenticator (no live browser/USB key required). |
+
+```bash
+# Single test file
+cargo test -p passkey-server --test smoke
+
+# Full suite
+cargo test -p passkey-server
+```
+
+Integration tests assume a live `DATABASE_URL` (per `bun run dev:stack`).
+
+## Container build
+
+`Dockerfile` is a 4-stage cargo-chef build (chef → planner → builder → runtime); `RUST_VERSION=1.88` (matches CI toolchain pin in `.github/workflows/rust.yml`); `CARGO_CHEF_VERSION=0.1.77`; final image is `debian:bookworm-slim` + the `passkey-server` binary. The build does **not** require a live `DATABASE_URL` because the committed `.sqlx/` cache satisfies the SQLx macros at compile time.
+
+The image is pushed to `<env>acr.azurecr.io/passkey-server` by `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` and rolled out to the `passkey-server` Container App. See [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) §9.2 (CI) and §10 (deployment).
+
+## Cross-references
+
+-   [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) — full system reference (anchor sections noted above)
+-   [`CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md) — Repository Invariants (SQLx offline mode, Redis DB-0 / DB-1 split, webhook signature shape)
+-   [`crates/db/`](../../crates/db/) — SQLx repository layer
+-   [`packages/passkeys/`](../../packages/passkeys/) — TypeScript SDK that talks to this server (the `@deficom/passkeys` package)
+-   [`packages/database/prisma/schema.prisma`](../../packages/database/prisma/schema.prisma) — `PasskeyUser` + `PasskeyCredential` model definitions (Prisma owns the schema; SQLx reads it)

 ## API reference
 
-| Method | Path                                    | Description                                   |
-| ------ | --------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
-| `GET`  | `/health`                               | Liveness check                                |
-| `GET`  | `/api/resolve?name=<ens-name>`          | Resolve ENS name to address                   |
-| `GET`  | `/api/score?user=<address or ENS name>` | Compute off-chain score (no on-chain publish) |
-| `POST` | `/api/updateScore`                      | Compute score and publish to `OffchainScores` |
-| `GET`  | `/api/ccip/{sender}/{call_data}`        | EIP-3668 gateway                              |
-| `GET`  | `/api-docs`                             | Swagger UI                                    |
-| `GET`  | `/api-docs/openapi.json`                | Raw OpenAPI spec                              |
+| Method | Path                                    | Access    | Rate limit                         | Description                                   |
+| ------ | --------------------------------------- | --------- | ---------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
+| `GET`  | `/health`                               | public    | none                               | Liveness check                                |
+| `GET`  | `/api/resolve?name=<ens-name>`          | public    | 1 req per 3 s sustained · 20 burst | Resolve ENS name to address                   |
+| `GET`  | `/api/score?user=<address or ENS name>` | localhost | none                               | Compute off-chain score (no on-chain publish) |
+| `POST` | `/api/updateScore`                      | localhost | none                               | Compute score and publish to `OffchainScores` |
+| `GET`  | `/api/ccip/{sender}/{call_data}`        | public    | 1 req per 2 s sustained · 40 burst | EIP-3668 gateway                              |
+| `GET`  | `/api-docs`                             | public    | none                               | Swagger UI                                    |
+| `GET`  | `/api-docs/openapi.json`                | public    | none                               | Raw OpenAPI spec                              |
+
+**Access notes.** Routes marked `localhost` are gated by the `middleware::localhost::require_localhost` layer in `src/middleware/localhost.rs` — wired into a `local_routes` sub-router in `src/main.rs`, so they only respond to requests originating from the same host. This is the trust-boundary mechanism for `POST /api/updateScore` (which signs and broadcasts publish transactions) and `GET /api/score` (which is treated as an internal computation hook). External access goes through the public surface (`/api/resolve` for FE/CCIP-Read clients; `/api/ccip/*` for `TrustScore.getLatestScoreWithProof` callers). See [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) §11.1 for the trust-boundary framing.
+
+**Rate limits.** Encoded with `tower_governor` in `src/main.rs`. Note that `tower_governor`'s `per_second(n)` means "1 token added every n seconds" — not "n requests per second" — so `per_second(3)` is a sustained rate of 1 req per 3 s (≈20/min). The user-facing `/api/resolve` is the tighter limit; `/api/ccip/*` is looser because wallets auto-retry. Architecture.md §5.3 carries the same numbers.
 
 Swagger UI is available at `http://localhost:3004/api-docs` when the server is running locally.
